query: "Microsoft fiscal year 2024" revenue $ billion official earnings
answer: Microsoft's fiscal year 2024 revenue was $245.122 billion, a 15.67% increase from 2023. The company's net income for 2024 was $92.8 billion.

results.title: FY24 Q3 - Press Releases - Investor Relations - Microsoft
results.snippet: ·        Net income was $21.9 billion and increased 20%. ·        Office Commercial products and cloud services revenue increased 13% (up 12% in constant currency) driven by Office 365 Commercial revenue growth of 15%. ·        Office Consumer products and cloud services revenue increased 4% and Microsoft 365 Consumer subscribers grew to 80.8 million. ·        Dynamics products and cloud services revenue increased 19% (up 17% in constant currency) driven by Dynamics 365 revenue growth of 23% (up 22% in constant currency). ·        Server products and cloud services revenue increased 24% driven by Azure and other cloud services revenue growth of 31%. ·        Windows revenue increased 11% with Windows OEM revenue growth of 11% and Windows Commercial products and cloud services revenue growth of 13% (up 12% in constant currency). ·        Xbox content and services revenue increased 62% (up 61% in constant currency) driven by 61 points of net impact from the Activision acquisition.

results.title: Microsoft's Revenue Hits Record $262 Billion, Net Income Soars to ...
results.snippet: # 📈 Microsoft’s Revenue Hits Record $262 Billion, Net Income Soars to $93 Billion in 2024. 📈 Microsoft’s Revenue Hits Record $262 Billion, Net Income Soars to $93 Billion in 2024. Microsoft posted strong financial results in 2024, with revenue reaching $261.8 billion (up 12.9% year-over-year) and net income rising to $92.8 billion (up 14.5%), resulting in a net profit margin of 35.4%. Since 1995, the company has maintained a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 12.9% in revenue and 14.5% in net income, underscoring its consistent long-term growth and profitability. ##### Where Data Tells the Story. ###### Home. ###### Support. ###### Creators. ###### Legal. 📈 Microsoft’s Revenue Hits Record $262 Billion, Net Income Soars to $93 Billion in 2024 - Voronoi.

results.title: FY24 Q2 - Performance - Investor Relations - Microsoft
results.snippet: Revenue increased $9.3 billion or 18% driven by growth across each of our segments. Cost of revenue increased $2.1 billion or 12% driven by growth in Microsoft Cloud and Gaming, offset in part by a decline in Devices. Gross margin increased $7.1 billion or 20% driven by growth across each of our segments. Excluding the impact of the prior year change in accounting estimate for the useful lives of our server and network equipment, gross margin percentage increased 2 points driven by improvement in More Personal Computing. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate, Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage increased 1 point driven by improvements in Azure and Office 365 Commercial, offset in part by the impact of scaling our AI infrastructure. Operating expenses increased $505 million or 3% driven by Gaming, with 11 points of growth from the Activision Blizzard acquisition, offset in part by 7 points of favorable impact from the prior year Q2 charge.

results.title: Microsoft made $64.7 billion in revenue in Q4 2024 fiscal year - Neowin
results.snippet: # Microsoft made $64.7 billion in revenue in Q4 2024 fiscal year. ##### Microsoft generated $64.7 billion in revenue for the fiscal year Q4 2024 time period, up 15 percent from the same period a year ago. For its fiscal year fourth quarter 2024 time period that ended on June 30, Microsoft had $64.7 billion in revenue. That was up by 10 percent compared to the $20.1 billion in net income Microsoft got from the same period a year ago. That's because Microsoft stated that revenues from Azure and other cloud services went up by 29 percent during the quarter. Revenues from the company's Productivity and Business Processes divisions were $20.3 billion for the quarter, up by 11 percent from the same period a year ago. Revenues from Microsoft's More Personal Computing divisions were $15.9 billion for the quarter, up by 14 percent from a year ago. ### Microsoft shares detailed guide to meet Windows 11 TPM requirements when moving VMs. Apple CEO Tim Cook on stage.
